interactions.effect: Increased bleeding risk — garlic has antiplatelet properties
interactions.recommendation: Monitor INR closely; consider reducing dose or avoiding high-dose garlic supplements

interactions.effect: Reduces saquinavir plasma levels by up to 54%
interactions.recommendation: Avoid high-dose garlic supplements with saquinavir

interactions.effect: Reduces non-heme iron absorption by 25–70%
interactions.recommendation: Take iron supplements 2+ hours apart from green tea

interactions.effect: Green tea contains vitamin K; may reduce warfarin effectiveness
interactions.recommendation: Monitor INR; keep green tea intake consistent

interactions.effect: Additive stimulant effect — increased heart rate, blood pressure, anxiety
interactions.recommendation: Limit green tea if taking stimulant medications

interactions.effect: Thujone is pro-convulsant at high doses; may reduce anticonvulsant effectiveness
interactions.recommendation: Contraindicated in seizure disorders; avoid with anticonvulsants

interactions.effect: May increase neurotoxic effects of thujone
interactions.recommendation: Avoid alcohol during wormwood protocol

interactions.effect: May increase bleeding risk
interactions.recommendation: Monitor if on anticoagulants

interactions.effect: Eugenol has antiplatelet properties; increased bleeding risk
interactions.recommendation: Monitor INR closely

interactions.effect: High-dose eugenol may affect hepatic metabolism
interactions.recommendation: Avoid exceeding recommended doses

interactions.effect: Reduces iron absorption
interactions.recommendation: Take iron 2+ hours apart

interactions.effect: Antimicrobial activity may kill beneficial bacteria
interactions.recommendation: Take probiotics 2+ hours apart from oregano oil

interactions.effect: Inhibits CYP2D6 and CYP3A4; increases levels of many medications
interactions.recommendation: Consult physician; may need dose adjustments

interactions.effect: Additive blood glucose lowering — risk of hypoglycemia
interactions.recommendation: Monitor blood glucose; metformin dose may need reduction

interactions.effect: Increases albendazole blood levels (may be desirable for systemic parasites)
interactions.recommendation: Can be used intentionally for systemic parasites; monitor liver enzymes

interactions.effect: Increases praziquantel levels by ~50%
interactions.recommendation: Can be used intentionally for combined parasite treatment

interactions.effect: Increases albendazole levels
interactions.recommendation: Monitor liver enzymes

interactions.effect: Significantly increased risk of Stevens-Johnson syndrome / toxic epidermal necrolysis
interactions.recommendation: DO NOT COMBINE

interactions.effect: Reduced mebendazole levels
interactions.recommendation: May need extended treatment course

interactions.effect: Vitamin K antagonizes warfarin's anticoagulant effect
interactions.recommendation: Keep vitamin K intake consistent; don't suddenly increase or decrease

interactions.effect: Additive antiplatelet effect; slightly increased bleeding risk
interactions.recommendation: Generally safe at 2–3 g/day; monitor at higher doses

interactions.effect: Additive blood pressure lowering
interactions.recommendation: Monitor blood pressure; may allow medication dose reduction

interactions.effect: Additive blood pressure lowering
interactions.recommendation: Monitor blood pressure

interactions.effect: Risk of selenium toxicity (>400 mcg/day) — 2 Brazil nuts = ~200 mcg
interactions.recommendation: Limit to 1–2 Brazil nuts/day; don't combine with selenium supplements

interactions.effect: May adsorb medications, reducing absorption
interactions.recommendation: Take medications 2+ hours apart from diatomaceous earth

interactions.effect: Adsorbs medications and supplements, reducing absorption by 30–80%
interactions.recommendation: Take ALL medications/supplements 2–4 hours apart from activated charcoal

severity_levels.Severe: Combination may cause serious harm; avoid or closely monitor
severity_levels.Mild: Be aware; usually manageable with timing adjustments

metadata.note: This is not exhaustive. Always consult a pharmacist or physician for your specific medication list.
